mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales (RSAFS), measured as a three-month moving average, indicates strong consumer spending momentum. This robust demand supports corporate revenues and profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns after rising retail sales are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window, or if the signal yields Rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, the monetary tightening reduces market liquidity and increases discount rates applied to equity valuations. This compression multiplies negative effects on stock prices, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If in periods when FEDFUNDS > 5.0, the 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative (one-tailed t-test p > 0.10), or if the signal's hit rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts (HOUST) year-over-year combined with a rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals a severe housing market downturn that weakens economic growth and financial stability. This joint occurrence predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the interaction signal of declining HOUST and rising FEDFUNDS does not produce negative 20-day returns in at least 60% of cases, or Rank IC of the combined signal < 0.04 over 2 years.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime where the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is below 5% (tight labor market), an increase in the unemployment rate signals the onset of labor market softening, which reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If in the low unemployment regime, increases in UNRATE do not lead to negative 60-day returns significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.10) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which boosts corporate earnings and overall economic activity. This positive supply-side environment leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return following industrial production increases is not positive with statistical significance (Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) exceed 1.7 million units annualized, it indicates an overbuilt housing stock that will eventually lead to price declines and reduced construction activity. This oversupply scenario predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If when housing starts exceed 1.7M, the 5-day forward return is not negative in at least 55% of instances over a 3-year rolling evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth (e.g., monthly gains > 200,000) simultaneously with a rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) suggests the economy is overheating. The central bank's aggressive tightening to cool inflation leads to lower equity valuations, predicting negative forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the joint signal of strong employment growth and rising rates does not produce negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or if the interaction term in a linear regression is not statistically significant (p < 0.10) over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a high inflation regime (CPI year-over-year above 3%), any increase in the federal funds rate amplifies negative market sentiment because investors anticipate further aggressive tightening. This monetary tightening cycle compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rise by at least 25bp in a month
failure_condition: If in the high inflation regime, the negative forward returns following Fed funds increases are not significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.10), or if the signal's cumulative PL is negative over a 3-year period.
